### Post by anas2009 on 2010-01-06
i installl ddclient and work perfect ubuntu 9.04
the problem that ddclient.conf is not protected
anyone use my computer can access to ddclient.conf and see my user and pass
# Configuration file for ddclient generated by debconf
#
# /etc/ddclient.conf
pid=/var/run/ddclient.pid
protocol=dyndns2
use=web, web=checkip.dyndns.org/,web-skip='ip adress'#found after IP
server=members.dyndns.org
login=XXXX
password='XXXXXX
XXXXX
 
 
there is a way to protect the file please or encryppt pssword in ddclient.conf???
regards.

---

### Post by BkkBonanza on 2010-01-06
Change the permissions to read/write only by the user that runs ddclient. And other users none.
eg. if bob runs ddclient (or whoever, maybe root?)

chown bob:bob /etc/ddclient.conf
chmod 600 /etc/ddclient.conf

Now only bob can read/write the file.

You can get fancier depending on what you want, maybe admin group can also read etc.

---

### Post by FuturePilot on 2010-01-06
Default looks like it's only rw by root.

```
sudo chown root:root /etc/ddclient.conf
sudo chmod 600 /etc/ddclient.conf
```

---
